title: LoRA vs Full Fine-tuning: An Illusion of Equivalence
summary: This study investigates the equivalence of LoRA and full fine-tuning by analyzing intruder dimensions and forgetting patterns. The results show that LoRA introduces intruder dimensions, leading to less forgetting than full fine-tuning, but forgetting is reversible with a simple intervention. However, in continual learning setups, LoRA's forgetting increases over time, making it less effective for long-term learning.
